title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Lamb Ouzi Recipe | Authentic Whole Slow Roasted Lamb
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Lamb Ouzi, a whole slow roasted Middle Eastern delicacy. This detailed recipe ensures tender, flavorful lamb perfect for any feast.
longTailKeywords: Lamb Ouzi whole slow roasted recipe
lowCompetitionKeywords: traditional Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi dinner
seo.h1: Authentic Lamb Ouzi: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Lamb Ouzi
Step-by-Step Instructions for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
Pro Tips for the Best Lamb Ouzi Results
Variations and Substitutions for Lamb Ouzi
seo.imageAlt: A beautifully plated dish of authentic Lamb Ouzi, whole slow roasted to perfection, garnished with nuts and herbs.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich flavors of the Middle East with this incredible whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi, a true feast for the senses!

description: Lamb Ouzi is a majestic Middle Eastern dish featuring tender, slow-roasted lamb infused with aromatic spices, served over a bed of flavorful rice. This celebratory meal brings together succulent meat and fragrant grains, making it a centerpiece for any special occasion. Prepare to impress your guests with this unforgettable culinary masterpiece.

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb and Marinade
instructions.text: Pat the lamb shoulder or leg dry. In a small bowl, combine 1 tablespoon of Ouzi spice blend, cinnamon, cardamom, nutmeg, black pepper, and 1 teaspoon of salt. Rub this spice mixture thoroughly over the entire lamb. Drizzle with 2 tablespoons of olive oil. Cover and let marinate in the refrigerator for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to penetrate deep into the meat.

instructions.title: Sear and Prepare for Roasting
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 325F (160C). Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot over medium-high heat. Sear the marinated lamb on all sides until beautifully browned, about 3-4 minutes per side. Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside. In the same pot, add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Slow Roast the Lamb
instructions.text: Return the seared lamb to the pot with the onions. Pour in 2 cups of the chicken or lamb broth. Bring the liquid to a simmer, then cover the pot tightly with a lid or aluminum foil. Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and slow roast for 3.5 to 4 hours, or until the lamb is fork-tender and easily pulls away from the bone. Baste occasionally with the pan juices to keep it moist. Once cooked, carefully remove the lamb from the pot and let it rest, covered, while you prepare the rice.

instructions.title: Prepare the Ouzi Rice
instructions.text: While the lamb is resting, drain the soaked basmati rice. In a separate large pot, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the rinsed and drained basmati rice to the pot, along with the remaining 1 tablespoon of Ouzi spice blend and a pinch of salt. Stir gently for 2 minutes to toast the rice and coat it with spices.

instructions.title: Cook the Rice and Combine with Peas
instructions.text: Pour in the remaining 4 cups of chicken or lamb broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender. Once the rice is cooked, gently fold in the frozen peas. Let the rice rest, covered, for 5 minutes before fluffing with a fork.

instructions.title: Assemble and Serve
instructions.text: To serve, spread the fragrant Ouzi rice on a large serving platter. Carefully place the slow-roasted lamb on top of the rice. Garnish generously with toasted pine nuts, slivered almonds, and fresh chopped parsley. You can shred the lamb directly onto the rice or serve it as a whole piece for a grand presentation. Serve immediately and enjoy this magnificent Middle Eastern feast.

tips: For even deeper flavor, make small incisions in the lamb before marinating and stuff with garlic slivers.
If you don't have a specific Ouzi spice blend, a good Middle Eastern 7-spice blend is an excellent substitute, or you can mix your own with common spices.
For the best presentation, toast the nuts just before serving to ensure they are warm and crunchy.
article:
Unlock the secrets to a truly unforgettable Middle Eastern feast with a dish that embodies celebration, warmth, and incredible flavor: Lamb Ouzi. This isn't just a meal; it's a culinary event, a centerpiece that brings families and friends together. Imagine succulent, fall-off-the-bone lamb, slow-roasted to perfection, nestled atop a bed of fragrant, spiced rice studded with toasted nuts. This traditional Middle Eastern dinner recipe is a testament to the power of slow cooking, transforming simple ingredients into an aromatic symphony that will transport your taste buds straight to the heart of the Levant. While it might seem daunting, mastering whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi is a rewarding journey that yields spectacular results, perfect for special occasions or when you simply crave an extraordinary home-cooked meal.

## What is Lamb Ouzi?

Lamb Ouzi, often spelled Ouzi or Ouzy, is a grand, festive dish deeply cherished across the Middle East, particularly in countries like Syria, Lebanon, Jordan, and Iraq. At its core, it's a magnificent presentation of slow-roasted lamb, typically a whole leg or shoulder, combined with a rich, aromatic rice pilaf. Unlike some stews or quick preparations, Lamb Ouzi is a dish of patience and tradition. The lamb is marinated, then roasted for many hours until it's incredibly tender, infused with spices, and practically melting in your mouth. The accompanying rice is a masterpiece in itself, cooked with a blend of warming spices like allspice, cinnamon, and cardamom, and often enriched with sautéed ground meat (though not always in the whole roasted version) and a generous scattering of toasted nuts like pine nuts and almonds. It's more than just food; it's a symbol of hospitality and generosity, frequently served at weddings, holidays, and large family gatherings, making it one of the most celebrated Middle Eastern dinner recipes.

## Ingredients You Need for Lamb Ouzi

Crafting an authentic whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi requires a thoughtful selection of ingredients. Here's what you'll need to create this magnificent dish:

For the Lamb:
*   **A whole lamb leg or shoulder (bone-in, 5-7 lbs):** Bone-in adds immense flavor and moisture during the long cooking process.
*   **Marinade:**
    *   1 cup plain full-fat yogurt
    *   4-6 cloves garlic, minced
    *   1/4 cup olive oil
    *   2 tablespoons lemon juice
    *   2 teaspoons salt
    *   1 teaspoon black pepper
    *   1 teaspoon ground allspice
    *   1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon
    *   1/2 teaspoon ground cardamom
    *   Optional: a pinch of saffron threads steeped in a little hot water

For the Aromatic Rice:
*   3 cups basmati rice, rinsed thoroughly
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   2-3 tablespoons olive oil or ghee
*   1 lb ground lamb or beef (optional, for a richer pilaf)
*   1 teaspoon ground allspice
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon
*   1/4 teaspoon ground cardamom
*   1/4 teaspoon black pepper
*   1/4 cup pine nuts
*   1/4 cup slivered almonds
*   5 cups chicken or lamb broth
*   Salt to taste

For Garnish:
*   Fresh parsley, chopped
*   Toasted pine nuts and almonds (extra)
*   Pomegranate seeds (optional, for color and tang)

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

The heart of a truly exceptional Lamb Ouzi lies in its slow-roasted lamb and perfectly cooked rice. Follow these steps for a glorious outcome:

1.  **Prepare the Lamb Marinade:** In a large bowl, whisk together the yogurt, minced garlic, olive oil, lemon juice, salt, pepper, allspice, cinnamon, and cardamom (and saffron if using).
2.  **Prepare the Lamb:** Pat the lamb leg or shoulder dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, score the surface of the lamb deeply in a diamond pattern, about 1/2 inch deep. This allows the marinade to penetrate deeply. Rub the marinade all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into the scores.
3.  **Marinate:** Place the marinated lamb in a large roasting pan or a heavy-duty plastic bag. Cover tightly and refrigerate for at least 8 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to meld.
4.  **Roast the Lamb:**
    *   Preheat your oven to 325°F (160°C).
    *   Remove the lamb from the refrigerator and let it come to room temperature for about an hour before roasting.
    *   Place the lamb in a sturdy roasting pan, fat side up. You can add a cup of water or broth to the bottom of the pan to help keep the lamb moist.
    *   Cover the roasting pan tightly with foil.
    *   Roast for 3-4 hours, then remove the foil and continue roasting for another 1-2 hours, or until the lamb is incredibly tender and easily pulls apart with a fork. Baste occasionally with the pan juices. The internal temperature should reach 190-200°F (88-93°C) for maximum tenderness.
5.  **Rest the Lamb:** Once cooked, remove the lamb from the oven, transfer it to a cutting board, and tent it loosely with foil. Let it rest for at least 20-30 minutes before carving or shredding. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a succulent result.
6.  **Prepare the Aromatic Rice:**
    *   While the lamb is resting, heat olive oil or ghee in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.
    *   Add the pine nuts and slivered almonds and toast until golden brown. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
    *   If using ground meat, add it to the pot and cook, breaking it up, until browned. Drain any excess fat.
    *   Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.
    *   Stir in the rinsed basmati rice, allspice, cinnamon, cardamom, and black pepper. Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, to lightly toast the rice and infuse it with spices.
    *   Pour in the chicken or lamb broth and season with salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.
    *   Remove from heat and let sit, covered, for 10 minutes. Fluff with a fork.
7.  **Assemble and Serve:** On a large serving platter, spread the aromatic rice. Carefully place the whole slow roasted lamb (or shredded lamb, if preferred) on top of the rice. Garnish generously with fresh chopped parsley, extra toasted pine nuts, almonds, and pomegranate seeds if using.

## Pro Tips for the Best Lamb Ouzi Results

Achieving that perfect, fall-off-the-bone tender lamb and fluffy, flavorful rice for your Lamb Ouzi requires a few key insights:

*   **Quality Lamb is Key:** Start with a good quality, fresh lamb leg or shoulder. The bone-in cut adds depth of flavor and helps keep the meat moist during the long cooking time.
*   **Marinate Longer, Not Shorter:** The longer the lamb marinates, the more flavor it absorbs. Overnight is ideal, allowing the yogurt and spices to tenderize and infuse the meat deeply.
*   **Low and Slow Roasting:** This is non-negotiable for tender lamb. Resist the urge to crank up the heat. A consistent low temperature (325°F/160°C) over several hours breaks down the tough connective tissues, resulting in unbelievably tender meat.
*   **Don't Skip the Rest:** Resting the lamb after roasting is crucial. It allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb the juices, ensuring a succulent and moist result when you carve or shred it.
*   **Toast Your Nuts:** Lightly toasting the pine nuts and almonds for the rice pilaf brings out their nutty flavor and adds a delightful textural contrast. Watch them carefully, as they can burn quickly.
*   **Rinse the Rice Thoroughly:** Rinsing basmati rice removes excess starch, preventing it from becoming sticky and ensuring a fluffy, separate grain texture.
*   **Taste and Adjust Spices:** While the spice blend provided is traditional, feel free to adjust to your preference. Some like a stronger cinnamon note, others more cardamom. Taste the ground meat and rice mixture before adding broth.

## Serving Suggestions for Lamb Ouzi

A grand dish like Lamb Ouzi deserves equally delightful accompaniments. Here are some traditional and complementary serving suggestions:

*   **Yogurt Sauce (Labneh or Plain):** A simple bowl of plain yogurt or creamy labneh, perhaps seasoned with a little salt, dried mint, and a drizzle of olive oil, provides a refreshing counterpoint to the rich lamb and rice.
*   **Simple Green Salad:** A crisp salad with a light lemon-tahini or olive oil dressing cuts through the richness and adds freshness.
*   **Middle Eastern Pickles (Torshi):** Assorted pickled vegetables, such as cucumbers, turnips, or wild cucumbers, offer a tangy, briny crunch that complements the savory flavors beautifully.
*   **Fresh Flatbread:** Warm pita bread or other Middle Eastern flatbreads are perfect for scooping up any extra rice or lamb.
*   **Tomato and Cucumber Salad:** A simple diced salad with fresh herbs like parsley and mint, dressed with lemon juice and olive oil, adds vibrant color and zest.

## Variations and Substitutions for Lamb Ouzi

While the traditional whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi is a masterpiece, there are several ways to adapt it to your preferences or what you have on hand:

*   **Meat Options:** If a whole lamb leg is too much, you can use a smaller lamb shoulder, lamb shanks, or even large lamb chops, adjusting roasting times accordingly. Some recipes use boneless lamb, but bone-in provides superior flavor.
*   **Rice Variations:** While basmati is classic, you could experiment with other long-grain rice varieties or even freekeh (roasted green wheat) for a different texture and nutty flavor.
*   **Spice Adjustments:** Feel free to tweak the spice blend. Some regions might add a touch of turmeric for color, or a pinch of white pepper. For a spicier kick, a tiny bit of cayenne pepper can be included in the marinade.
*   **Nut Alternatives:** If pine nuts or almonds are unavailable or not preferred, walnuts or pistachios can be used. For an allergy-friendly option, omit nuts entirely.
*   **Vegetable Additions:** Some cooks add chopped carrots, peas, or bell peppers to the rice pilaf for added nutrition, color, and sweetness.
*   **Stuffed Ouzi:** A more intricate variation involves deboning the lamb leg, stuffing the cavity with the spiced rice mixture, then roasting it. This requires more skill but results in an impressive presentation.
*   **Individual Portions:** For smaller gatherings, you can make individual Lamb Ouzi servings by wrapping smaller portions of spiced rice and shredded lamb in phyllo pastry and baking until golden.

Embark on this culinary journey and bring the magic of Lamb Ouzi to your table. Its deep flavors, aromatic spices, and tender lamb make it more than just a meal; it’s an experience that celebrates the rich heritage of Middle Eastern cuisine.
